terms.definition: Naive Bayes classifier for continuous features using Normal class-conditional likelihoods N(x_j; μ_{k,j}, σ²_{k,j}) per feature j and class k, combined with class priors. Canonical home for this likelihood choice in classical ML.
P(x_j | y=k) modeled as a Normal PDF with parameters μ_{k,j} and σ²_{k,j} estimated from training data in class k. Defined canonically in this topic; PDF formula in Common Distributions.
Model that specifies P(x|y) (and P(y)) then derives P(y|x) via Bayes' rule. GNB is generative; logistic regression is discriminative.
log P(y=k) + Σ_j log N(x_j; μ_{k,j}, σ²_{k,j}); GNB predicts the class with the highest score. Log form prevents underflow from multiplying many PDF values.
Approximation P(x|y) ≈ ∏_j P(x_j|y) treating features as conditionally independent given the class. Full treatment in Conditional Independence Assumption.
Small constant added to estimated σ² (e.g. sklearn var_smoothing) so Gaussian PDFs remain finite when a feature has near-zero variance within a class.
GaussianNB attribute storing fitted per-class per-feature means μ_{k,j} after fit — shape (n_classes, n_features).
